entries.rationale: REH.AX in uptrend (fast MA $15.10 > slow MA $14.48, spread 4.3%). Price pulled back 6.0% from recent high of $16.64 to $15.64. Trend active for 59 bars. Volume 0.8x avg. ATR=0.62, stop at $14.39.
AGL.AX in uptrend (fast MA $10.21 > slow MA $9.40, spread 8.7%). Price pulled back 7.3% from recent high of $10.60 to $9.83. Trend active for 13 bars. Volume 0.7x avg. ATR=0.23, stop at $9.36.
NXT.AX in uptrend (fast MA $13.80 > slow MA $13.27, spread 3.9%). Price pulled back 6.9% from recent high of $14.34 to $13.35. Trend active for 24 bars. Volume 0.9x avg. ATR=0.58, stop at $12.19.
ADH.AX in uptrend (fast MA $1.87 > slow MA $1.83, spread 2.1%). Price pulled back 7.0% from recent high of $2.01 to $1.87. Trend active for 17 bars. Volume 0.5x avg (LOW). ATR=0.08, stop at $1.71.

PDN.AX in uptrend (fast MA $13.27 > slow MA $12.34, spread 7.5%). Price pulled back 4.1% from recent high of $14.14 to $13.56. Trend active for 54 bars. Volume 0.5x avg (LOW). ATR=0.64, stop at $12.27.
ANZ.AX in uptrend (fast MA $39.77 > slow MA $37.68, spread 5.5%). Price pulled back 3.8% from recent high of $40.89 to $39.33. Trend active for 46 bars. Volume 0.8x avg. ATR=0.85, stop at $37.62.
ORA.AX in uptrend (fast MA $2.24 > slow MA $2.17, spread 3.2%). Price pulled back 7.3% from recent high of $2.34 to $2.17. Trend active for 9 bars. Volume 1.2x avg. ATR=0.08, stop at $2.01.

UPS is oversold with RSI=30.2 (threshold 35) and Z-score=-2.74 (threshold -2.0). Price $102.36 is 2.7 std devs below 20-day mean of $114.83. Volume surge 1.5x avg (capitulation +conf). Target reversion to $107.44 (+1.5x ATR), stop at $93.89.

ON is oversold with RSI=30.4 (threshold 35) and Z-score=-2.60 (threshold -2.0). Price $56.87 is 2.6 std devs below 20-day mean of $67.27. Volume 1.2x avg. Target reversion to $61.31 (+1.5x ATR), stop at $49.47.

exits.details: CHTR 2-day confirmed reversal: fast MA $232.10 crossed below slow MA $233.91. Close=$232.24, held -3 days.

factors.label: New Iranian supreme leader selected AND signals willingness to negotiate
Confirmed backchannel talks (Oman/Qatar mediation, both sides participating)
Trump publicly signals desire for off-ramp / deal with Iran
House passes War Powers Resolution to limit Iran operations
Key US coalition partner publicly withdraws support / calls for ceasefire
UN Security Council passes binding ceasefire resolution
search_keywords: UN security council iran ceasefire resolution

factors.label: Iran conventional military capability severely degraded (missiles, drones, air defence)
factors.source: CENTCOM Gen. Caine: missiles -86%, drones -73% as of March 4

factors.label: IRGC Navy substantially destroyed / port infrastructure degraded
factors.source: White House: 20+ ships sunk incl top submarine, zero Iranian warships in Gulf

factors.label: US ground troops deployed into Iran or neighbouring territory for invasion
Kurdish militias or PKK open second front against Iran / Turkish involvement
Hezbollah launches major offensive (hundreds of rockets, multi-front attack)
factors.source: Rockets on Haifa, IDF ground troops in Lebanon, 60 Hezbollah sites struck

factors.label: Houthis actively attacking Red Sea shipping (Bab el-Mandeb disrupted)
factors.source: ISW March 3, UK Parliament Briefing March 5, Wikipedia Red Sea crisis: 'As of 4 March, hostilities have not resumed — internal Houthi debate over response'

factors.label: Sudden spike in US military casualties triggers domestic political backlash
NATO formally invoked or NATO member directly engages militarily
factors.source: Reuters/NYT/Bloomberg March 4 2026: NATO air defences destroyed Iranian ballistic missile targeting Turkey/Incirlik — first direct NATO engagement

factors.label: US officials publicly state conflict duration exceeds 4 weeks
factors.source: Hegseth March 4 Pentagon briefing: war could last 3, 4, 6, or 8 weeks
factors.description: Longer stated timeline = longer supply disruption = lower ceasefire probability in near term

factors.label: Senate kills War Powers Resolution — Congress signals support for operations
factors.source: Senate vote 47-53 March 4, War Powers Resolution defeated

factors.label: US retail gasoline exceeds $5/gallon — political pressure on administration
factors.source: AAA March 5: national avg .11/gal — well below  threshold. California highest at .74. GasBuddy  source was erroneous.

factors.label: MAGA base splits on Iran war — visible Republican dissent in Congress
factors.source: AP/TIME March 4-5 2026: Massie R-KY leads House war powers bill; 2 senators broke party lines; broad MAGA dissent

factors.label: Trump approval collapses below 40% directly tied to Iran war
factors.source: YouGov/Economist poll March 2 2026: 38% approval second term low / Daily Beast March 4 2026

factors.label: Iranian regime collapses / is overthrown — new government signals ceasefire
Assassination or personal attack on US official / ally — retaliation cycle escalates
factors.source: Hegseth: killing of Trump assassination plot leader, 'Trump got the last laugh'

factors.label: Brent crude sustains above $100/barrel — economic pain triggers political pressure
factors.source: Argus Media + BusinessToday March 10 2026: Brent drops to 8 (-26% from Monday 00 high), not sustained above 00 for multiple days; Trump waiving oil sanctions

factors.label: US Navy establishes formal convoy escort system through Strait of Hormuz
War risk insurance premiums return to normal / Lloyd's reopens cover
Qatar resumes LNG deliveries to Asia / restarts energy diplomacy with Iran
Recession fears spike (PMI <45, unemployment rising) — pressure to end war
factors.source: NYT/CNBC/Guardian March 6 2026: -92k Feb jobs, unemployment 4.4%, Dow -800pts, recession fears spike tied to Iran war oil surge

factors.label: Gulf producers hitting storage limits — forced production shutdowns
factors.source: Reuters March 4 2026 — Iraq cut 1.1mb/d due to storage limits; IEA confirms forced production shutdowns across Gulf
factors.description: Shipping problem becomes supply destruction — harder to restart, deepens crisis. JPMorgan: 25-day limit from Feb 28 = watch ~March 20-25.

factors.label: Gulf producers actively pressuring US/Iran to reopen Hormuz due to storage
factors.source: Reuters/FT/BBC/Al Jazeera March 6 2026: Qatar Energy Minister Saad al-Kaabi warns war will force all Gulf exports to halt within weeks, predicts 50/bbl — active pressure on US/Iran to reopen Hormuz
factors.description: Economic self-interest of Gulf states creates diplomatic pressure for resolution

leaderboard.name: Lower Band Reversion
leaderboard.description: IBS-based lower band reversion (Quantitativo LBR). Published Sharpe 2.11 on SPY. On individual stocks: Sharpe -2.08. Classic ETF-to-stock adaptation failure.

leaderboard.name: Mean Reversion
leaderboard.description: RSI(14) + z-score mean reversion on individual SP500 stocks. Core active strategy. Enters oversold reversals with ATR-based stop losses. Optimized via coordinate descent to Sharpe 1.04, CAGR 15.69% (v

leaderboard.name: Trend Following
leaderboard.description: Fast/slow MA crossover trend following with pullback entries. Core active strategy. Enters higher-probability pullbacks within confirmed uptrends.

leaderboard.name: Opening Gap
leaderboard.description: Gap-and-fill strategy targeting gap-down opens with IBS confirmation. Core active strategy. Short holding periods (2-7 days).

leaderboard.name: Sector Rotation
leaderboard.description: Top-down momentum sector rotation. Selects strongest GICS sectors by momentum, buys strongest stocks within them. Passes solo (Sharpe 0.43) but degrades combined portfolio.

leaderboard.name: Bollinger Band Squeeze
leaderboard.description: Bollinger Band squeeze: enters when bands contract (low volatility) and break out. Captures volatility expansion moves.

leaderboard.name: Short Term MR
leaderboard.description: RSI(2)/IBS short-term mean reversion. Captures rapid 1–5 day reversals that standard MR (RSI 14) misses.

leaderboard.name: Momentum Breakout
leaderboard.description: N-day high breakout momentum entry. Enters at point of price breach rather than lagging MA crossover.

leaderboard.name: Triple RSI
leaderboard.description: Triple RSI (modified Connors R3): RSI(5)<30 + 3 consecutive declining days. Published 90% WR, PF 4.0 on SPY. Testing on individual stocks in Wave 3.

leaderboard.name: ConnorsRSI2
leaderboard.description: ConnorsRSI2 mean reversion strategy designed for ETFs. Fails on individual SP500 stocks. Not viable as-designed — see ETF Adaptation Fails pattern.

leaderboard.name: MTF Momentum
leaderboard.description: Multi-timeframe momentum: weekly trend confirmation + daily entry timing. Combines fast and slow momentum signals.

leaderboard.name: Adx Trend Pullback
leaderboard.description: ADX > 25 (strong trend) + pullback to 20-EMA. Enter on bounce from EMA. Exit: trailing ATR stop.
leaderboard.reference: Welles Wilder 'New Concepts in Technical Trading' (1978)

leaderboard.name: Consecutive Down Days
leaderboard.description: Buy after N consecutive down days in an uptrend. Statistical mean-reversion pattern with 60-70% historical win rate on indices.

leaderboard.name: Demark Sequential
leaderboard.description: TD Sequential buy setup: 9 consecutive closes below close 4 bars earlier. Enter on bar 9. Exit on TD sell setup or time.
leaderboard.reference: Tom DeMark 'The New Science of Technical Analysis' (1994)

leaderboard.name: Donchian Breakout
leaderboard.description: Buy on 20-day high breakout, sell on 10-day low. Classic trend following. ATR position sizing.

leaderboard.name: Gap And Go
leaderboard.description: Buy stocks that gap UP > 2% at open with volume confirmation. Ride momentum. Exit: intraday trailing stop or close.
leaderboard.reference: Quantified Strategies gap research, related to Opening Gap

leaderboard.name: Heikin Ashi Reversal
leaderboard.description: 3+ red Heikin-Ashi candles followed by green doji/reversal in uptrend. Enter long. Exit on 2 red HA candles.
leaderboard.reference: Japanese candlestick patterns, quantified by Quantified Strategies

leaderboard.name: Inside Bar Nr7
leaderboard.description: NR7 (narrowest range of 7 days) or inside bar → breakout entry. Enter on break of NR7 high/low. Exit: trailing stop or time-based (3-5 days).
leaderboard.reference: Toby Crabel 'Day Trading with Short Term Price Patterns' (1990)

leaderboard.name: Keltner Reversion
leaderboard.description: Price touches lower Keltner Channel (EMA ± ATR mult) → buy. Exit at middle band (EMA). Uptrend filter.
leaderboard.reference: Chester Keltner (1960), modernized by Linda Bradford Raschke

leaderboard.name: Macd Divergence
leaderboard.description: Price makes new low but MACD histogram makes higher low. Enter long. Exit on MACD crossover or time stop.

leaderboard.name: Monthly Rotation
leaderboard.description: Monthly rebalance: rank sectors/stocks by 6-month momentum. Hold top N. Rotate monthly. Cash filter: below SMA-200 → cash.
leaderboard.reference: Faber 'A Quantitative Approach to TAA' (2007), Antonacci dual momentum

leaderboard.name: Overnight Return
leaderboard.description: Buy at close, sell at open. Captures overnight premium. Filter: strong recent performers only.

leaderboard.name: Pead Earnings Drift
leaderboard.description: Post-Earnings Announcement Drift. Buy after positive earnings surprise, hold 20-60 days. Needs earnings data.

leaderboard.name: Put Call Vix Proxy
leaderboard.description: VIX > 30 or VIX spike > 20% in 1 day → buy SPY/broad market. Exit when VIX drops below 20. Contrarian sentiment play.
leaderboard.reference: VIX fear gauge research, CBOE put/call ratio studies

leaderboard.name: Relative Strength Pullback
leaderboard.description: Stocks with relative strength rank > 80th percentile that pull back to 10-EMA. Enter on bounce. Exit: trailing stop.
leaderboard.reference: O'Neil CANSLIM (1988), Minervini 'Trade Like a Stock Market Wizard'

leaderboard.name: Rsi Divergence
leaderboard.description: Price makes new low but RSI makes higher low (bullish divergence). Enter long. Exit on RSI > 60 or time.

leaderboard.name: Stochastic Oversold
leaderboard.description: Stochastic %K < 20 and %D < 20 in uptrend (>SMA200). Exit on %K > 80 or time stop.

leaderboard.name: Volume Climax
leaderboard.description: Extreme volume spike (>3x avg) on a down day in uptrend = capitulation selling. Buy reversal. Exit: time or strength.
leaderboard.reference: Quantified Strategies volume research, Wyckoff method

leaderboard.name: Vwap Reversion
leaderboard.description: Price > 2 std below daily VWAP in uptrending stock. Enter long. Exit at VWAP or above. Needs intraday-proxy via daily estimate.

leaderboard.name: Williams Percent R
leaderboard.description: Williams %R oversold (<-80) with trend filter. Exit on %R > -20 or time stop.

patterns.name: ETF Strategy Adaptation Fails
patterns.summary: ConnorsRSI2 and LBR designed for ETFs fail on individual stocks. Don't adapt ETF strategies to stocks.

patterns.name: Fee Drag at Low Equity
patterns.summary: At $4K equity, Moomoo fees ($1.10/trade) eat 74% of gross profit. Need ~$10K for viable Moomoo or switch to Alpaca ($0 c

patterns.name: Position Slot Contention
patterns.summary: All 4 dormant strategies fail combined portfolio due to slot contention at max_positions=10. Need allocation pools befor

patterns.name: SMA-200 Filter Win
patterns.summary: Biggest filter win: +0.28 Sharpe improvement, promoted to v2.1 config.

patterns.name: VIX Filter Counterproductive
patterns.summary: VIX filter is counterproductive for MR-heavy portfolio because MR needs high-VIX panic periods.

hypotheses.title: RSI period transfer test
hypotheses.hypothesis: RSI(2) works for ConnorsRSI2, does it also work for Williams %R?

learnings: autoresearch: keep — Disable SMA200 filter — trade in all conditions
autoresearch: keep — Asymmetric risk: 1.5x stop, 3x target
autoresearch: keep — RSI period 8 — between grid points
autoresearch: keep — Disable both SMA200 and earnings — max signal count

pending_items.title: OOS validation of reoptimized SP500 config

daily_insight.title: Fee drag: 0.8pp CAGR lost to real trading costs
daily_insight.subtitle: Moomoo US: ~$1.09/order · 7 trades filtered

discoveries.detail: Volume filter improves MR signal quality
